text.arabic:
الْحَمْـدُ للهِ الَّذي أَطْعَمَنـي هـذا
وَرَزَقَنـيهِ مِنْ غَـيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِنِّي وَلا قُوَّة
text.transliteration:
Alhamdu lillāhi ‘l-ladhī aṭ`amanī hādhā,
wa razaqanīhi min ghayri ḥawlin minnī wa lā quwwah.

translations.en:
Praise is to Allah Who has given me this food,
and sustained me with it though I was unable to do it and powerless.

items.reference:
At-Tirmidhi, Abu Dawud, and Ibn Majah. See also Al-Albani, Sahih
At-Tirmidhi 3/159.

text.arabic:
الْحَمْـدُ للهِ حَمْـداً كَثـيراً طَيِّـباً مُبـارَكاً فيه،
غَيْرَ مَكْفِيٍّ وَلا مُوَدَّعٍ وَلا مُسْتَغْـنىً عَنْـهُ رَبَّـنا
text.transliteration:
Alhamdu lillāhi ḥamdan kathīran tayyiban mubārakan fīh,
ghayra makfiyyin wa lā muwadda`in,
wa lā mustaghnan `anhu Rabbanā.

translations.en: All praise is to Allah, praise in abundance, good and blessed. It cannot [be compensated for, nor can it] be left, nor can it be done without, our Lord.
